title: Agrostis scribneriana
taxonomy: Agrostis scribneriana is a species of grass in the family Poaceae, commonly known as the Scribner's bentgrass. It is a perennial grass native to the western United States. The species is classified in the genus Agrostis, which contains over 200 species of grasses. Agrostis scribneriana is further classified within the subfamily Pooideae, which includes many temperate grass species. This grass is often found growing in wet meadows and along streams.

intro: Agrostis scribneriana, also known as Scribner's bentgrass, is a species of grass native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever reduction and cough suppression, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Agrostis scribneriana include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Scribner's bentgrass include teas and infusions, which are often used to relieve respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
